name: Pittsburgh (United States)

colors.symbolism: from the personal arms of William Pitt, 1st Earl of Chatham — the city's namesake
from the personal arms of William Pitt, 1st Earl of Chatham — the city's namesake

description: A vertical triband of black, gold (yellow), and black, with the city's coat of arms in the centre on the gold stripe.
history: Adopted on 15 March 1899 by ordinance of the Pittsburgh City Council. The black-and-gold colour scheme is taken from the personal arms of William Pitt, 1st Earl of Chatham, namesake of the city — and is now also the colour scheme of all of Pittsburgh's major sports teams. In 2004 the North American Vexillological Association ranked it the 24th-best of 150 American city flags surveyed and the top-ranked tricolour.

features.symbolism: black and gold from the personal arms of William Pitt, 1st Earl of Chatham, namesake of the city; the same colour scheme used by Pittsburgh's major sports franchises

features.role: coat of arms of the City of Pittsburgh
features.symbolism: the city's coat of arms — based on the arms of William Pitt — is placed at the centre of the gold stripe
